title: Callbacks from C to R
text: callback C shape: callback signature follows call-signature format used dyncall().
code: int add(int x, int y); add <- ccallback("ii)i", function(x, y) x + y) dyncall(add, "ii)i", 20L, 3L) #> [1] 23

title: Callbacks from C to R
text: callback pointer just another foreign function pointer. can passed back call signature expects pointer. example small, shows lifetime rule larger APIs: keep factorial_ptr reachable R long C may call .
code: factorial_callback <- function(x, fun) {     if (x < 2L) {         1L     } else {         x * dyncall(fun, "ip)i", x - 1L, fun)     } }  factorial_ptr <- ccallback("ip)i", factorial_callback) dyncall(factorial_ptr, "ip)i", 6L, factorial_ptr) #> [1] 720

title: Callbacks from C to R
text: qsort() classic C API accepts comparison callback: comparison callback receives pointers two elements. R callback reads double values unpack(). callback object must remain assigned compare_ptr qsort() returns. APIs store callbacks beyond registration call, store callback object somewhere lifetime foreign registration.
code: void qsort(void *base, size_t nmemb, size_t size,            int (*compar)(const void *, const void *)); libc_names <- c("msvcrt", "c", "c.so.6") libc <- new.env(parent = globalenv()) dynbind(libc_names, "qsort(pLLp)v;", envir = libc) #> dynbind report #>   library: libc.so.6 #>   unresolved symbols: 0  compare_double <- function(px, py) {     x <- unpack(px, 0L, "d")     y <- unpack(py, 0L, "d")     if (x < y) {         -1L     } else if (x > y) {         1L     } else {         0L     } }  set.seed(42) x <- rnorm(12L) expected <- sort(x) compare_ptr <- ccallback("pp)i", compare_double)  libc$qsort(x, length(x), 8L, compare_ptr) #> NULL all.equal(x, expected) #> [1] TRUE

title: Callbacks from C to R
text: Callbacks can receive return registered aggregate types value <Type> syntax used dyncall(). Aggregate arguments arrive raw-backed struct objects, field access uses $ helpers ordinary cdata() objects. Aggregate return callbacks must return raw-backed object exact registered type.
code: cstruct("Point{dd}x y;")  point <- cdata(Point) point$x <- 1.5 point$y <- 2.25  point_sum <- ccallback("<Point>)d", function(p) {     p$x + p$y })  dyncall(point_sum, "<Point>)d", point) #> [1] 3.75 make_point <- ccallback("dd)<Point>", function(x, y) {     out <- cdata(Point)     out$x <- x     out$y <- y     out })  returned <- dyncall(make_point, "dd)<Point>", 4.5, 5.25) c(returned$x, returned$y) #> [1] 4.50 5.25

title: Callbacks from C to R
text: Callback lifetime depends whether C calls pointer current function call stores later. qsort() comparatively safe finishes control returns R. Event loops riskier C may call pointer long registration function returned.

title: Callbacks from C to R
text: Callbacks cross C R, mistakes severe ordinary R function errors. callback signature must match exact C callback type. Keep callback object alive R foreign code may call . let C call callback R state cleaned . Avoid throwing errors foreign event loops. Handle errors inside callback return C-level failure value API supports one. aggregate callback returns, return raw-backed struct object exact expected type size. mismatch disables callback. Aggregate callbacks require implemented 64-bit x86 ARM64 dyncallback backend; unsupported platforms fail callback created.

title: Callbacks from C to R
text: APIs register callbacks, keep small R object owns foreign handle callback pointer. important part exact wrapper shape; callback pointer remains reachable least long C library can use .
code: make_registration <- function(handle) {     state <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())     state$handle <- handle     state$callback <- ccallback("ip)v", function(code, userdata) {         # Handle the event and return a C-compatible value.         invisible(NULL)     })      foreign_register_callback(handle, state$callback)     state }

title: dynbind and DynPort bindings
text: dynbind() takes library name handle library signature. installs thin R wrappers environment. generated wrapper still calls resolved C function rdyncall. wrapper just hides address signature user-facing call.
code: math_names <- c("msvcrt", "m", "m.so.6") math <- new.env(parent = globalenv()) info <- dynbind(     math_names,     paste(         "sqrt(d)d",         "cos(d)d",         "sin(d)d",         sep = ";"     ),     envir = math )  c(     sqrt = math$sqrt(49),     cos = math$cos(0),     sin = math$sin(pi / 2) ) #> sqrt  cos  sin  #>    7    1    1

title: dynbind and DynPort bindings
text: return value reports library handle unresolved symbols. makes possible fail early platform lacks function require. exploratory bindings, unresolved optional symbols can reported user. required bindings, fail exposing partially working wrappers.
code: str(info$unresolved.symbols) #>  chr(0)

title: dynbind and DynPort bindings
text: Character library names look like file paths loaded directly. character values resolved dynfind(). Use vector candidate names cross-platform bindings. example, Windows may use msvcrt, Unix-like systems commonly expose C runtime symbols c c..6.
code: libc_names <- c("msvcrt", "c", "c.so.6") libc_handle <- dynfind(libc_names) is.nullptr(libc_handle) #> [1] FALSE

title: dynbind and DynPort bindings
text: dynport() reads DCF .dynport file generates real R package rdyncall-managed library. generated package namespace populated metadata DynPort file. default DynPort library : Generated package names use option rdyncall.dynport.package.prefix, whose default value "dyn.", plus Package field DynPort file.
code: dynport_lib() #> [1] "/home/runner/.cache/R/rdyncall/dynports/R-4.6.1"

title: dynbind and DynPort bindings
text: minimal DynPort file records package name, library candidates, function bindings, optionally constants aggregate types. package ships one current-format DynPort file: inst/dynports/SDL3.dynport. generated SDL3 headers porter kept package realistic, non-toy binding example. C libraries, generate .dynport file porter pass dynport(portfile = ...). regeneration script SDL3 follows shape SDL3 header directory known:
code: Package: SDL3 Version: 3.4.10 Library:     SDL3     SDL3-0     SDL3-3 Function:     SDL_GetPlatform()Z; library(porter)  sdl3_header <- "/path/to/include/SDL3/SDL.h" sdl3_include <- dirname(dirname(sdl3_header))  header <- tempfile(fileext = ".h") writeLines("#include <SDL3/SDL.h>", header)  sdl3 <- port(header,     limit = dirname(sdl3_header),     cflags = paste0("-I", sdl3_include) ) sdl3 <- port_set(sdl3,     Package = "SDL3",     Version = "3.4.10",     Library = c("SDL3", "SDL3-0", "SDL3-3") )  port_write(sdl3, "inst/dynports/SDL3.dynport")

title: dynbind and DynPort bindings
text: RDYNCALL_ARTICLE_EXTERNAL=true SDL3 available, article builds loads generated package bundled DynPort, calls non-GUI platform query. chunk open window enter event loop. normal local rendering, RDYNCALL_ARTICLE_EXTERNAL can stay unset false; SDL3 code shown skipped. pkgdown workflow sets true, installs SDL3 system libraries, therefore executes non-GUI probe CI. SDL3 installed, code remains visible article skipped unless external article execution explicitly requested.
code: sdl3_names <- c("SDL3", "SDL3-0", "SDL3-3") Sys.setenv(RDYNCALL_ARTICLE_EXTERNAL = "true") portfile <- system.file("dynports", "SDL3.dynport",                         package = "rdyncall", mustWork = TRUE) lib <- tempfile("rdyncall-dynport-lib")  generated <- dynport(portfile = portfile, package = "dyn.SDL3Article",                      lib = lib, rebuild = TRUE, quiet = TRUE) generated #> [1] "dyn.SDL3Article"  getExportedValue(generated, "SDL_GetPlatform")() #> [1] "Linux"

title: dynbind and DynPort bindings
text: Use dyncall() single function address small experiment. Use dynbind() know small set functions want ordinary R wrappers one environment. Use dynport() binding metadata live data file load generated R package. three layers use underlying signatures, start getting C declarations signatures correct.

title: Getting started with rdyncall
text: basic workflow three steps: Find load shared library. Resolve function symbol. Call address signature matches C function type. C math function declared : corresponding rdyncall call signature "d)d": one double argument, ), double return value. signature match real C function type, process can crash. Keep C declaration beside R binding develop.
code: double sqrt(double x); math_names <- c("msvcrt", "m", "m.so.6") mathlib <- dynfind(math_names) sqrt_addr <- dynsym(mathlib, "sqrt") dyncall(sqrt_addr, "d)d", 144) #> [1] 12

title: Getting started with rdyncall
text: dynbind() creates thin R wrappers one functions library. wrapper still uses signature internally, call site becomes ordinary R code.
code: math <- new.env(parent = globalenv()) dynbind(math_names, "sqrt(d)d;", envir = math) #> dynbind report #>   library: libm.so.6 #>   unresolved symbols: 0  math$sqrt(625) #> [1] 25

what: Call an R function through a C callback pointer
title: Getting started with rdyncall
text: ccallback() turns R function C function pointer. signature describes callback function type. Keep R reference callback objects long foreign code may call . callback garbage-collected C still holds pointer, next C call can crash R.
code: add <- ccallback("ii)i", function(x, y) x + y) dyncall(add, "ii)i", 20L, 3L) #> [1] 23

what: Describe and use a C struct
title: Getting started with rdyncall
text: C aggregate types registered compact structure signatures. C type: can registered used R: cdata() returns raw-backed object struct metadata. Field access reads writes raw bytes according registered layout.
code: struct Rect {   short x;   short y;   unsigned short w;   unsigned short h; }; cstruct("ArticleRect{ssSS}x y w h;")  rect <- cdata(ArticleRect) rect$x <- 40L rect$y <- 60L rect$w <- 10L rect$h <- 15L  rect$w * rect$h #> [1] 150

title: Getting started with rdyncall
text: Use signatures translate C declarations rdyncall signatures. Use structs, unions, memory aggregate layouts raw memory access. Use callbacks C API stores later calls R function pointer. Use dynbind dynport larger bindings.

title: Signatures for C calls
text: Type signatures describe individual C values. common ones : Pointer types written *, *int *. Typed aggregate pointers written *<TypeName>. Registered aggregate values passed value written <TypeName>.

title: Signatures for C calls
text: call signature form: example: becomes:
code: argument-types ) return-type double sqrt(double x); d)d

title: Signatures for C calls
text: table shows translation pattern several APIs used articles demos. following example calls C’s strlen, declared : Z passes C string L receives size_t result unsigned 64-bit-compatible numeric value R’s side.
code: size_t strlen(const char *s); libc_names <- c("msvcrt", "c", "c.so.6") libc <- new.env(parent = globalenv()) dynbind(libc_names, "strlen(Z)L;", envir = libc) #> dynbind report #>   library: libc.so.6 #>   unresolved symbols: 0  libc$strlen("rdyncall") #> [1] 8

title: Signatures for C calls
text: R shared library can also bound. useful examples R available wherever package loaded. R_rsort(double *x, int n) mutates R numeric vector place first argument pointer vector data.
code: rapi <- new.env(parent = globalenv()) dynbind("R", "R_pow(dd)d; R_rsort(pi)v;", envir = rapi) #> dynbind report #>   library: libR.so #>   unresolved symbols: 0  rapi$R_pow(2, 10) #> [1] 1024  x <- c(3.5, 1.25, 8.0, -2.0) rapi$R_rsort(x, length(x)) #> NULL x #> [1] -2.00  1.25  3.50  8.00

title: Signatures for C calls
text: dynbind() accepts library signature: semicolon-separated list function bindings. Whitespace around function bindings ignored, longer signatures can formatted readability.
code: math_names <- c("msvcrt", "m", "m.so.6") math <- new.env(parent = globalenv()) dynbind(     math_names,     paste(         "sqrt(d)d",         "cos(d)d",         "sin(d)d",         sep = ";"     ),     envir = math ) #> dynbind report #>   library: libm.so.6 #>   unresolved symbols: 0  c(     sqrt = math$sqrt(81),     cos = math$cos(0),     sin = math$sin(pi / 2) ) #> sqrt  cos  sin  #>    9    1    1

title: Signatures for C calls
text: C functions declared ... need dyncall_variadic(). fixed parameters return type go signature; actual vararg types call site go varargs. Default C promotions responsibility. example, variadic float argument passed promoted double.
code: libc_names <- c("msvcrt", "c", "c.so.6") printf_addr <- dynsym(dynfind(libc_names), "printf") dyncall_variadic(     printf_addr,     "Z)i",     varargs = "i",     "value = %d\n",     42L )

title: Signatures for C calls
text: Start C declaration, guessed R value. Translate every fixed argument order. Put ) return type. Use pointer signatures output buffers mutable arrays. Keep callback struct lifetimes explicit. Test tiny inputs calling code allocates, frees, opens files, enters event loop.

what: Raw memory with pack() and unpack()
title: Structs, unions, and memory
text: lowest-level tools pack() unpack(). write read C values inside raw vectors memory referenced external pointers. Offsets byte offsets starting 0.
code: buf <- raw(8) pack(buf, 0, "f", 1.5) #> NULL pack(buf, 4, "f", 2.25) #> NULL  c(     first = unpack(buf, 0, "f"),     second = unpack(buf, 4, "f") ) #>  first second  #>   1.50   2.25

title: Structs, unions, and memory
text: Use highest-level interface still matches C API binding.

title: Structs, unions, and memory
text: C type: represented one structure signature: object returned cdata() raw vector class metadata. Field access translated byte-level reads writes.
code: struct Rect {   short x;   short y;   unsigned short w;   unsigned short h; }; cstruct("DocRect{ssSS}x y w h;") DocRect #> struct typeinfo DocRect #>   size: 8 #>   align: 2 #>   signature: ssSS #>   fields: #>  name type offset array_len #>     x    s      0         1 #>     y    s      2         1 #>     w    S      4         1 #>     h    S      6         1  rect <- cdata(DocRect) rect$x <- 10L rect$y <- 20L rect$w <- 200L rect$h <- 100L  rect #> struct DocRect { #>    x :10  #>    y :20  #>    w :200  #>    h :100  #>  }

title: Structs, unions, and memory
text: Array lengths written field type. whole array field assigned read R vector.
code: cstruct("DocColor{C[4]}rgba;")  color <- cdata(DocColor) color$rgba <- c(255L, 128L, 0L, 255L) color$rgba #> [1] 255 128   0 255

title: Structs, unions, and memory
text: Struct fields can contain registered aggregate types, including fixed-size arrays nested structs. Nested structs still raw-backed values. matters passed value C functions supported platforms.
code: cstruct("DocVec2{ff}x y;") cstruct("DocSegment{<DocVec2>[2]}points;")  a <- cdata(DocVec2) a$x <- 1.25 a$y <- 2.5  b <- cdata(DocVec2) b$x <- 3.5 b$y <- 4.75  segment <- cdata(DocSegment) segment$points <- list(a, b) segment$points #> [[1]] #> struct DocVec2 { #>    x :0  #>    y :0  #>  } #>  #> [[2]] #> struct DocVec2 { #>    x :0  #>    y :0  #>  }

title: Structs, unions, and memory
text: Bitfields keep ordinary integer storage type type list put bit width field-name list. Unnamed bitfields :4 reserve padding bits. zero-width unnamed bitfield, written :0, aligns next bitfield new storage unit.
code: cstruct("DocBits{IIII}enabled:1 mode:3 :4 code:8;")  bits <- cdata(DocBits) bits$enabled <- 1L bits$mode <- 5L bits$code <- 171L  c(     enabled = bits$enabled,     mode = bits$mode,     code = bits$code ) #> enabled    mode    code  #>       1       5     171

title: Structs, unions, and memory
text: Layout directives appear field list. @packed equivalent @pack(1). @pack(n) caps member alignment n, @align(n) raises final aggregate alignment least n.
code: cstruct("DocPacked{Cd}tag value @packed;") cstruct("DocPack4{Cd}tag value @pack(4);") cstruct("DocAligned{C}tag @align(8);")  c(     packed_size = DocPacked$size,     packed_align = DocPacked$align,     pack4_align = DocPack4$align,     aligned_align = DocAligned$align ) #>   packed_size  packed_align   pack4_align aligned_align  #>             9             1             4             8

title: Structs, unions, and memory
text: Unions use | instead { type name. union fields share storage. Writing one field changes another field reads bytes.
code: cunion("DocValue|iC[4]}i bytes;")  value <- cdata(DocValue) value$i <- 16909060L value$bytes #> [1] 4 3 2 1

title: Structs, unions, and memory
text: Register aggregate layout using dyncall(). Keep C definition beside R signature developing. careful platform-dependent layout, especially packing bitfields. Treat external pointers borrowed memory unless C API explicitly says R owns must free pointer.

what: Improved Foreign Function Interface and Dynamic Bindings to C Libraries
title: Improved Foreign Function Interface and Dynamic Bindings to C Libraries
text: rdyncall provides R interface DynCall libraries. low-level Foreign Function Interface (FFI) loading shared C libraries, resolving symbols, calling C functions R signature, working C struct union data, exposing R functions C callback pointers. package intended developers already know C API want call need exploratory dynamic binding layer R without writing compiled wrapper every function.

title: Improved Foreign Function Interface and Dynamic Bindings to C Libraries

code: remotes::install_github("hongyuanjia/rdyncall")

title: Improved Foreign Function Interface and Dynamic Bindings to C Libraries
text: Call C function directly loading library, resolving symbol, providing call signature: signature "d)d" means one C double argument C double return value. Signatures must match target C function type. R functions can also wrapped C callback pointers: Foreign aggregate layouts described used raw-backed objects:
code: library(rdyncall)  mathlib <- dynfind(c("msvcrt", "m", "m.so.6")) sqrt_addr <- dynsym(mathlib, "sqrt")  dyncall(sqrt_addr, "d)d", 144) add <- ccallback("ii)i", function(x, y) x + y) dyncall(add, "ii)i", 20L, 3L) cstruct("Rect{ssSS}x y w h;")  rect <- cdata(Rect) rect$x <- 40 rect$y <- 60 rect$w <- 10 rect$h <- 15  rect$w

title: Improved Foreign Function Interface and Dynamic Bindings to C Libraries
text: pkgdown articles organized short learning path: Getting started introduces basic load-resolve-call workflow. Signatures C calls shows translate C declarations rdyncall signatures. Structs, unions, memory covers raw buffers, aggregate layouts, bitfields, packed/aligned data. Callbacks C R explains callback pointers lifetime rules. dynbind DynPort bindings shows move one-calls generated binding packages.

title: Improved Foreign Function Interface and Dynamic Bindings to C Libraries
text: dynload(), dynunload(), dynsym(), dynpath(), dyncount() dynlist() load shared libraries inspect symbols. dynfind() resolves common short library names across platforms package manager locations. dyncall() dyncall_variadic() call C functions using compact type signatures. dynbind() creates thin R wrappers group C functions. cstruct(), cunion(), cdata() .ctype() describe access C aggregate data. pack() unpack() read write low-level C values raw vectors memory referenced external pointers. ccallback() turns R function C function pointer. dynport() builds loads generated R packages DCF .dynport binding specifications.

title: Improved Foreign Function Interface and Dynamic Bindings to C Libraries
text: rdyncall can model ordinary C struct union layouts supports several layout features needed real C APIs: fixed-size array fields, written type[N], C[4]; integer bitfields, written field-name list, flags:3; packed aligned layouts via @packed, @pack(n) @align(n); nested aggregate fields -value aggregate calls supported DynCall backends. Callback signatures currently support aggregate -value arguments returns.

title: Dynamic wrapping of R functions as C callbacks — ccallback
text: Callbacks user-defined functions registered foreign library executed later time within library. Examples include user-interface event handlers registered GUI toolkits, , comparison functions custom data types passed generic sort algorithm. function ccallback() wraps R function fun C function pointer returns external pointer. foreign C function type wrapped R function specified call signature given signature. C function pointer called, global callback handler (implemented C) executed first, dynamically creates R call expression fun using arguments, passed C converted R, according argument types signature within call signature specified. See dyncall() details format. Finally, handler evaluates R call expression within environment given envir. return, R return value fun coerced C value, according return type signature specified signature . Aggregate -value callback arguments returns use <Type> signature syntax dyncall(). aggregate argument passed R callback raw-backed cdata object struct typeinfo attributes. aggregate return value must raw-backed object aggregate type size. Type storage mismatches disable callback emit warning. Aggregate callbacks supported implemented 64-bit x86 ARM64 dyncallback backends. unsupported backends, creating callback whose signature contains <Type> fails early. error occurs evaluation, callback disabled invocations. (behaviour might change future.)

title: Binding C library functions via thin call wrappers — dynbind
text: libnames character vector external pointer handle specifying shared library. Character values contain path separator, name existing file, passed directly dynload(). character values treated short library names loaded using dynfind(). External pointer handles returned dynload() dynfind() used directly. signature character string specifying library signature determines set foreign function names types. See details. envir environment use installation call wrappers. callmode character string specifying calling convention, see details. pattern NULL regular expression character string applied symbolic names. replace NULL replacement character string applied pattern part symbolic names. funcptr logical, indicates whether foreign objects refer functions (FALSE, default) function pointer variables (TRUE rarely needed). x S3 dynbind.report object print. ... additional arguments passed base::print() methods.

title: Binding C library functions via thin call wrappers — dynbind
text: function returns list two fields: libhandle External pointer returned dynload(). unresolved.symbols vector character strings, names unresolved symbols. side effect, wrapper, dynbind() assigns function-name  corresponding call wrapper function environment given envir. shared library found, error reported.

title: Binding C library functions via thin call wrappers — dynbind
text: dynbind() makes set C functions available R installation thin call wrappers. set functions, including symbolic name function type, specified signature; character string encodes library signature: library signature compact plain-text format specify set function bindings. consists function names corresponding call signature. Function bindings separated ";" (semicolon); white spaces (including tab new line) allowed semicolon.   example specifies three function bindings OpenGL library:   Symbolic names resolved using library specified libnames. Character short library names loaded using dynfind(), character paths loaded directly using dynload(), external pointer handles returned dynload() dynfind() used -. function, thin call wrapper function created using following template:   <MODE> replaced callmode argument, see dyncall() details calling conventions. <TARGET> replaced external pointer, resolved function-name. <SIGNATURE> replaced call signature string contained signature. call wrapper installed environment given envir. assignment name obtained function signature. pattern replace given, text replacement applied name assignment, useful basic C name space mangling exchanging prefix. special case, dynbind() supports binding pointer--function variables, indicated setting funcptr TRUE, case <TARGET> replaced expression unpack(<TARGET>,"p",0) order dereference <TARGET> pointer--function variable call-time.
code: function-name ( call-signature ; ... `"glAccum(If)v ; glClear(I)v ; glClearColor(ffff)v ;"` function(...) .dyncall.<MODE> ( <TARGET>, <SIGNATURE>, ... )

title: Foreign Function Interface with support for almost all C types — dyncall
text: dyncall() offers flexible Foreign Function Interface (FFI) C language support calls arbitrary pre-compiled C function types run-time. Almost C fundamental argument- return types supported including extended support pointers. limitations given arity well. addition, Microsoft Windows 32-Bit Intel/x86 platform, supports multiple calling conventions interoperate System DLLs. Foreign C function types specified via plain text type signatures. foreign C function type target function known FFI advance, preparation foreign call via plain text type signature information. several advantages: R arguments need match exactly. Although R lacks fundamental C value types, supported via coercion interface (e.g. C float 64-bit integer). Arity argument type checks help make interface type-safe certain degree encourage end-users use interface interpreter prompt rapid application development. foreign function called specified address, external pointer obtained dynsym() getNativeSymbolInfo(). signature character string specifies formal argument--return types foreign function using call signature string. match function type foreign function given address, otherwise can lead fatal R process crash. calling convention specified explicitly via function dyncall() using callmode argument implicitly using dyncall.* functions. See details . package option rdyncall.callvm.size controls byte size internal dyncall CallVM argument stack. default 4096. Set option loading rdyncall; changing package load resize already-created CallVM objects. Arguments passed via ... converted C according signature; see details. dyncall_variadic() calls C functions declared .... signature argument describes fixed parameter prefix return type, varargs describes actual argument types passed ... specific call site. C default promotions caller's responsibility; example, pass promoted variadic float values double ("d"). Given signature matches foreign function type, FFI provides certain level type-safety users, exposing foreign functions via call wrappers done dynbind() dynport(). Several basic argument type-safety checks done preparation foreign function call: arity formals actual arguments must match must compatible well. Otherwise, foreign function call aborted error risking fatal system crash.

title: Foreign Function Interface with support for almost all C types — dyncall
text: Calling Conventions specify sub-routine calls performed, , arguments results passed, machine-level. differ significantly among families CPU Architectures well OS Compiler implementations. platforms, single "default" C Calling Convention used. exception, Microsoft Windows 32-Bit Intel/x86 platform several calling conventions common. C libraries still use "default" C (also known "cdecl") calling convention, working Microsoft System APIs DLLs, "stdcall" calling convention must used. follows description supported Win32 Calling Conventions: "cdecl" Dummy alias default "stdcall" C functions stdcall calling convention. Useful Microsoft Windows System Libraries (e.g. KERNEL32.DLL, USER32.DLL, OPENGL32.DLL ...). Third-party libraries usually prefer default C cdecl calling convention. "fastcall.msvc" C functions fastcall calling convention compiled Microsoft Visual C++ Compiler. rare usage. "fastcall.gcc" C functions fastcall calling convention compiled GNU C Compiler. rare usage. "thiscall" C++ member functions. "thiscall.gcc" C++ member functions compiled GNU C Compiler. "thiscall.msvc" C++ member functions compiled Microsoft Visual C++ Compiler. current version package practical reasons, callmode argument effect almost platforms, except R running Microsoft Windows 32-Bit Intel/x86 platform, dyncall uses specified calling convention. example, loading OpenGL across platforms, "stdcall" used instead "default", Windows, OpenGL System DLL. exceptional, cases, "default" ("cdecl", alias) need used normal C shared libraries Windows. stage development, support C++ calls considered experimental. Support Fortran planed yet implemented dyncall.

title: Foreign Function Interface with support for almost all C types — dyncall
text: implementation based dyncall library (part DynCall project). following processor architectures supported: X86 32- 64-bit, ARM v4t-v7 oabi/eabi (aapcs) armhf including support Thumb ISA, PowerPC 32-bit, MIPS 32- 64-Bit, SPARC 32- 64-bit. library built tested work various OSs: Linux, Mac OS X, Windows 32/64-bit, BSDs, Haiku, Nexenta/Open Solaris, Solaris, Minix Plan9, well embedded platforms Linux/ARM (OpenMoko, Beagleboard, Gumstix, Efika MX, Raspberry Pi), Nintendo DS (ARM), Sony Playstation Portable (MIPS 32-bit/eabi) iOS (ARM - armv6 mode ok, armv7 unstable). context R, dyncall currently support PowerPC 64-Bit.

title: Portable searching and loading of shared libraries — dynfind
text: libnames vector character strings specifying several short library names. auto.unload logical: TRUE finalizer registered closes library garbage collection. See dynload() details.

title: Portable searching and loading of shared libraries — dynfind
text: dynfind() returns external pointer (library handle), search successful. Otherwise, library located, NULL returned.

title: Portable searching and loading of shared libraries — dynfind
text: dynfind() offers platform-portable naming interface loading specific shared library. naming scheme standard locations shared libraries OS-specific. loading shared library dynamically run-time across platforms via standard interfaces dynload() dyn.load(), platform-test usually needed specify OS-dependant library file path. library name problem encountered via breaking library file path several abstract components:   permutation values component concatenation, list possible file paths can derived. dynfind() goes list try opening library. first success, search stopped function returns. Given three components location, prefix suffix set properly per OS basis, unique identification library given libname - short library name. libraries, multiple ‘short library name’ needed make mechanism work across major platforms. example, load Standard C Library across major R platforms:   Windows MSVCRT.dll loaded; libc.dylib Mac OS X; libc..6 Linux libc.BSD. sample list values three components: location: /usr/local/lib/, C:/Windows/System32/ prefix: lib (common), empty - common Windows suffix: .dll (Windows), .(ELF), .dylib (macOS) empty - useful platforms vector locations initialized dynamic linker search rules, including environment variables PATH Windows LD_LIBRARY_PATH Unix-flavour systems. dynamic linker lookup fails, dynfind() also checks library directories belong current R runtime, R.home("lib") R.home("bin"), common package-manager library locations Homebrew (HOMEBREW_PREFIX, /opt/homebrew, /usr/local), MacPorts (MACPORTS_PREFIX, /opt/local), Linuxbrew (/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew) Scoop (SCOOP, SCOOP_GLOBAL, ProgramData/scoop). (set hardcoded locations might expand change within next minor releases). file extension depends OS: .dll (Windows), .dylib (macOS), .(others). Mac OS X, search library includes ‘Frameworks’ folders well. happens normal library search procedure uses slightly different naming pattern separate search phase:   frameworksLocation vector locations /System/Library/ /Library/. dynfind() loads library via dynload() passing parameter auto.unload.
code: <location> <prefix> <libname> <suffix> lib <- dynfind(c("msvcrt", "c", "c.so.6")) <frameworksLocation> Frameworks/ <libname> .framework/ <libname>

title: Loading of shared libraries and resolving of symbols (Alternative Framework) — dynload
text: dynload returns external pointer libhandle success. Otherwise NULL returned, library found linkage failed. dynsym returns external pointer address success. Otherwise NULL returned, address invalid symbol found. dynunload always returns NULL. dynpath returns single string. dyncount returns single integer. dynlist returns character vector.

title: Loading of shared libraries and resolving of symbols (Alternative Framework) — dynload
text: dynload() loads shared library current R process using OS-specific dynamic linker interface. libname passed -directly dynamic linker thus given OS-specific notation - see details. success, handle library represented external pointer R objects returned, otherwise NULL. auto.unload TRUE, finalizer function registered unload library garbage collection via dynunload(). dynsym() looks symbol names loaded libraries resolves memory addresses returned external pointer R objects. Otherwise NULL returned. protect.lib TRUE, library handle protected resolved address external pointers unloading. dynpath() returns full path loaded library specified libhandle. dyncount() returns number symbols loaded library specified libhandle. dynlist() returns symbol names loaded library specified libhandle. dynunload() explicitly unreferences loaded library specified libhandle. Setting auto.unload protect.lib TRUE, libraries remain loaded long resolved symbols use, get automatic unloaded resolved symbols remain. Dynamic linkers usually hold internal link count, library can opened multiple times via dynload() - balanced number calls dynunload() decreases link count unload library . Similar functionality available via base::dyn.load() base::getNativeSymbolInfo(), except path names filtered automatic unloading libraries supported.

Windows XP:  HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\SafeDllSearchMode 1, current directory searched system Windows directories, directories PATH environment variable. default value 0 (current directory searched system Windows directories). first directory searched one directory containing image file used create calling process. allows private dynamic-link library (DLL) files associated process found without adding process's installed directory PATH environment variable. search path can altered using SetDllDirectory() function. solution recommended instead using SetCurrentDirectory() hard-coding full path DLL. path specified redirection file application, function searches module application's directory. module exists application's directory, LoadLibrary() function ignores specified path loads module application's directory. module exist application's directory, LoadLibrary() loads module specified directory. information, see Dynamic Link Library Redirection Windows SDK Documentation.

Byte offset calculations start 0 relative first byte atomic vectors data area. x atomic vector, bound check carried read/write access. Otherwise, x external pointer, C NULL pointer check.

title: Handling of foreign C fundamental data types — pack
code: # transfer double to array of floats and back, compare precision: n <- 6 input <- rnorm(n) buf <- raw(n*4) for (i in 1:n) {     pack(buf, 4 * (i - 1), "f", input[i]) }  output <- numeric(n) for (i in 1:n) {     output[i] <- unpack(buf, 4 * (i - 1), "f") } # difference between double and float difference <- output - input print(cbind(input, output, difference)) #>           input     output    difference #> [1,] -0.3872136 -0.3872136 -1.251252e-08 #> [2,] -0.7854327 -0.7854326  1.904251e-08 #> [3,] -1.0567369 -1.0567368  4.009650e-08 #> [4,] -0.7955414 -0.7955414  2.387001e-08 #> [5,] -1.7562754 -1.7562754  1.275035e-08 #> [6,] -0.6905379 -0.6905379  2.689148e-08

RDYNCALL_RAYLIB_CACHE: cache directory used raylib demo     downloads release asset.

title: Improved Foreign Function Interface and Dynamic Bindings to C Libraries — rdyncall-package
text: package intended developers know C API want call need exploratory dynamic binding layer R without writing compiled wrapper every function. Shared libraries can opened directly dynload() located short names dynfind(). Function addresses resolved dynsym() called dyncall() using compact type signatures. dyncall_variadic() supports C functions declared ... call-site vararg signature supplied explicitly. C aggregate data can described cstruct() cunion() accessed raw-backed cdata() objects. aggregate layout support includes ordinary struct union fields, fixed-size array fields, integer bitfields, packed layouts, manual alignment directives -value aggregate calls supported DynCall backends. Aggregate -value callback arguments returns supported implemented 64-bit x86 ARM64 dyncallback backends. R functions can wrapped C function pointers ccallback(). Keep R reference callback objects long foreign code may call . dynport() builds loads generated R packages DCF .dynport binding specifications. package ships SDL3 DynPort generated current headers porter; libraries can bound generating loading additional .dynport files.

title: Improved Foreign Function Interface and Dynamic Bindings to C Libraries — rdyncall-package
text: Load libraries inspect symbols dynload(), dynfind(), dynsym() dynlist(). Call C functions dyncall() dyncall_variadic(). Create batches thin wrappers dynbind(). Describe access C aggregates cstruct(), cunion() cdata(). Read write low-level values pack() unpack(). Wrap R functions C callbacks ccallback(). Generate packages DCF DynPort specifications dynport().

title: Allocation and handling of foreign C aggregate data types — cstruct
text: References foreign C data objects represented objects class 'struct'. Two reference types supported: External pointers returned dyncall() using call signature typed pointer return type signature pointers extracted result unpack() S3 struct $-operators. Internal objects, memory-managed R, allocated cdata(): atomic raw storage object returned, initialized length equal byte size foreign C data type. order access manipulate data fields foreign C aggregate data objects, $ $<- S3 operator methods can used. S3 objects class struct attribute struct set name typeinfo object, provides run-time type information particular foreign C type. run-time type information foreign C struct union types need registered via cstruct cunion functions. C data types specified sigs, signature character string. formats types described next: Structure type signatures describe layout aggregate struct C data types. Type Signatures used within field-types. Fixed-size array fields written type signature followed [N], example C[4] unsigned char[4] <Point>[2] two nested aggregate values. field-names consists space separated identifier names match number fields. Integer bitfields written name:width field name list. Unnamed padding bitfields use :width; zero-width alignment bitfields use :0. Optional layout directives can follow field names final semicolon.   example C struct type:   corresponding structure type signature string :   Bitfields keep ordinary type signature field-types put bit width next field name:   Packed manually aligned aggregate layouts can registered @packed, @pack(n) @align(n) directives, n must positive power two. @packed equivalent @pack(1), @pack(n) caps member alignment n, @align(n) raises final aggregate alignment least n.   Union type signatures describe components union C data type. Type signatures used within field-types. Fixed-size array fields use [N] suffix structure fields. field-names consists space separated identifier names match number fields. layout directives can follow union field names.   example C union type:   corresponding union type signature string :   .ctype() can used cast foreign C data reference different type. using external pointer reference, can lead quickly fatal R process crash - like C.
code: struct-name { field-types } field-names [layout-directives] ; struct Rect {   signed short x, y;   unsigned short w, h; }; "Rect{ssSS}x y w h;" "Flags{IIII}a:1 b:3 :4 c:8;" "Packed{Cd}c d @packed;" "Pack4{Cd}c d @pack(4);" "PackedAligned{Cd}c d @packed @align(8);" union-name | field-types } field-names [layout-directives] ; union Value {   int anInt;   float aFloat;   struct LongValue aStruct }; "Value|if<LongValue>}anInt aFloat aStruct;"

title: Allocation and handling of foreign C aggregate data types — cstruct
code: # Specify the following foreign type: # struct Rect { #     short x, y; #     unsigned short w, h; # } cstruct("Rect{ssSS}x y w h;") r <- cdata(Rect) print(r) #> struct Rect { #>    x :0  #>    y :0  #>    w :0  #>    h :0  #>  } r$x <- 40 r$y <- 60 r$w <- 10 r$h <- 15 print(r) #> struct Rect { #>    x :40  #>    y :60  #>    w :10  #>    h :15  #>  } str(r) #>  'struct' raw [1:8] 28 00 3c 00 ... #>  - attr(*, "struct")= chr "Rect" #>  - attr(*, "typeinfo")=List of 7 #>   ..$ name     : chr "Rect" #>   ..$ type     : chr "struct" #>   ..$ size     : int 8 #>   ..$ align    : num 2 #>   ..$ basetype : logi NA #>   ..$ fields   :'data.frame':	4 obs. of  8 variables: #>   .. ..$ name          : chr [1:4] "x" "y" "w" "h" #>   .. ..$ type          : 'AsIs' chr [1:4] "s" "s" "S" "S" #>   .. ..$ offset        : int [1:4] 0 2 4 6 #>   .. ..$ array_len     : int [1:4] 1 1 1 1 #>   .. ..$ bit_offset    : int [1:4] NA NA NA NA #>   .. ..$ bit_width     : int [1:4] NA NA NA NA #>   .. ..$ storage_offset: int [1:4] NA NA NA NA #>   .. ..$ storage_size  : int [1:4] NA NA NA NA #>   ..$ signature: chr "ssSS" #>   ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "typeinfo" #>  - attr(*, "typeinfo_env")=<environment: 0x55afdaef1c90>

title: Utility functions for working with foreign C data types — is.nullptr
text: logical value returned .nullptr() .externalptr(). .externalptr() offset_ptr() returns external pointer value. floatraw() .floatraw() return atomic vector type raw tagged class floatraw. floatraw2numeric returns numeric atomic vector.

title: Utility functions for working with foreign C data types — is.nullptr
text: .nullptr() tests external pointer given x represents C NULL pointer. .externalptr() returns external pointer data area atomic vector given x. external pointer holds additional reference x R object prevent garbage collection. .externalptr() tests object given x external pointer. floatraw() creates array capacity store n single-precision C float values. array implemented via base::raw() vector. .floatraw() coerces numeric vector single-precision C float vector. Values given x converted C float values stored R raw vector via pack(). function useful calling foreign functions expect C float pointer via dyncall(). floatraw2numeric() coerces C float (raw) vector numeric vector. ptr2str(), strarrayptr(), strptr() currently experimental. offset_ptr() creates new external pointer pointing x plus byte offset. x given external pointer, address increased offset, , x given atomic vector, address data (pointing offset zero) taken basis increased offset. returned external pointer protected (offered C function R_MakeExternalPtr) external pointer x.

title: Utility functions for working with foreign C data types — is.nullptr
code: is.nullptr(NULL) #> [1] FALSE  one <- as.externalptr(1) is.externalptr(one) #> [1] TRUE  floatraw(1) #> floatraw[1]: 0  floats <- as.floatraw(1:10) all.equal(floatraw2numeric(floats), 1:10) #> [1] TRUE

title: rdyncall 0.10.0
text: Restore package compilation current R toolchains. Update bundled dyncall source stop tracking generated static libraries. Replace deprecated/internal R C API usage public accessors possible. Support dynlist() macOS dyld shared cache libraries. Generate real -disk R packages DCF DynPort files via dynport(). Add dyncall_variadic() calling C variadic functions explicit call-site vararg signatures. Allow dynbind() accept direct library paths existing external pointer handles addition short library names. Improve dynfind() discovery libraries installed common package managers, including Homebrew, MacPorts, Linuxbrew Scoop. Improve dynfind() discovery current R runtime library avoid treating -named directories direct dynbind() library paths. Return nested aggregate fields $ raw-backed struct objects can reused field access aggregate -value calls. Fix cstruct() cunion() field parsing whitespace follows type signature. Store aggregate field names explicit typeinfo$fields$name column. Add dedicated print methods typeinfo, struct, ctype, dynbind.report floatraw objects. Add struct union bitfield layout, access, DynPort parsing, -value aggregate support. Add rdyncall.callvm.size option configure CallVM argument stack size package load. Add -value aggregate argument return support dyncall() registered struct union types supported dyncall backends, including ARM64 aggregate ABI handling. Support fixed-size array fields struct union type signatures via type[N] suffix. Add @packed, @pack(n) @align(n) layout directives cstruct(), cunion() DynPort aggregate definitions. Refresh roxygen-generated documentation package metadata renewed development. Generate NAMESPACE roxygen2 metadata instead maintaining hand. Modernize GitHub Actions checks across Linux, macOS, Windows, optional R-hub workflow extended platform checks.

title: rdyncall 0.9
text: Update: use latest dyncall version. Improve dynamic library search Windows. Update package refactoring per CRAN requirements.

title: rdyncall 0.8
text: Reimplement simplified version dynfind(). Add dynport: pcap. Minor documentation update SDL installation Solaris/OpenCSW. Fix ARMv7 Thumb-2 ISA SPARC-v7/v9, x86_64-x64 mips-o32 ABIs.

title: rdyncall 0.7.5
text: CRAN release: 2012-09-12 Add support new ARM ABI armhf (ARM hardfloat), tested Raspberry Pi (armv6) Efika MX Smartbook (armv7). Accept NULL typed pointer arguments. Add support *v (void*) return type signature dyncall(). Add support **c external pointers support strptrarray(). Change dynport containers R namespaces environment objects remove .Internal calls :: operators dynports; dynports unloaded via detach(dynport:<PORTNAME>). Improve finalizer management callbacks. Add backward compatibility R 2.10 legacy Mac OS X 10.4/PowerPC R port. Add dynports: csound, GLUT, glfw, EGL, glpk. Add demos: glpk, gles (partial work).

title: rdyncall 0.7.4
text: Update dyncall support SPARC 32/64 SunPro fixes SPARC 64-bit. Add dynport SDL_net. Add public helper function offsetPtr(). Fix R 2.14 namespace handling missing lazyData field Env.

title: rdyncall 0.7.3
text: Fix Fedora/x64 adding search path lib64 folder dynfind(). Add support Sun make; DynCall uses Makefile.embedded. Add SPARC SPARC64 support using GCC tool-chain. Add support amd64 using Solaris tool-chain. Add vignette “foreign library interface”. Fix Solaris/x64 adding search path amd64 folder dynfind(). Fix examples libm using m..6 besides m Unix, needed Debian 6 sid unstable.

title: rdyncall 0.7.2
text: Add win64/mingw64 support.

title: rdyncall 0.7.1
text: Minor Makevars fix parallel builds.

title: rdyncall 0.7.0
text: First upload CRAN.
